answer_text:
The hidden rule is: A panel is marked with a yellow star if and only if it contains at least one cone that touches the left edge of the panel and at least one cone that touches the right edge of the panel. Panels without a star do not have any cone touching both the left and right edges.

**Final Answer:**

The panels with yellow stars have cones that touch both the left and right edges of the panel, while non-star panels have cones that touch only one side (either left or right, but not both).<｜end▁of▁sentence｜>
